title: Naming Rules on SQLite
text: Naming Rules on SQLite The syntax for index names is: TableName _IDX N TableName , where N is the number of the index. The following rules apply: Letters, numbers, dollar signs ($), and underscores (_) are allowed, but the first character must be a letter. Uniface accepts only uppercase names in the application model definition. The same rules apply to field names as for tables. Related Links Naming Rules for Uniface Development Objects The name of a development object is its unique identifier. When naming development objects, use the allowed syntax for the object name. Avoid using words that are reserved for use within Uniface and your own environment, including the operating system and database. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
